## __Data Science: Capstone Part 1 (Lightning Talk #1): Pitch + Problem Statement__

## Overview

In the field of data science, good projects are practical. Your capstone project should be manageable and affect a real world audience. This might be a domain you are familiar with, a particular interest you have, something that affects a community you are involved in, or an area that relates to a field you wish to work in.

One of the best ways to test ideas quickly is to share them with others. A good data scientist has to be comfortable discussing ideas and presenting to audiences. That's why for Part 1 of your Capstone project, you'll be preparing a lightning talk on some potential interest areas and datasets.

This deliverable will provide you with guidance to help you select an awesome topic and begin to build a polished Capstone project.

__Goal__: Host a lightning talk presentation describing at **least three** project proposals, including associated data, problem statement, goals, approach, and basic EDA.

### REQUIREMENTS

1. Prepare a **slide deck** and host a 3-4 minute lightning talk on __at least three__ potential topics for your capstone project. For each topic, define all required areas

2. __For each Topic__:
    - Problem Statement 
    - Goals
    - Approach
    - Data Source(s)
    - Data Info. (basic EDA)

3. Dataset should have atleast __15k+__ rows and __10+__ columns. Your dataset should be approved by the IA's. 
- **Please do send them links of your  3 datasets to the IAs via Slack for it to be approved by __TBA__**

__PS: NLP/text related datasets are not allowed.__ 


### Note
- ___Remember, if you can't find data to support your topic, then you can't move forward.___


## Deliverable Format & Submission
1. Slide Presentation
2. Jupyter Notebook (Basic EDA, data dictionary, etc. for each topic)

- Your data should be approved by your IAs by __TBA__. (Submit Links to your data [Here](https://forms.gle/hNhfPs7ZfDvzgVQN6))  The earlier the better.
- Submit both deliverables on **Google Classroom** by __TBA__ 

**Jupyter Notebook**

Your Jupyter Notebook should contain the following elements:
- Your Data Science Problem Statement for **each topic** (Not mandatory to have it here but recommended)
- Data Source for **each topic** (Mandatory)
- Data Dictionary for **each topic** (Mandatory)
- EDA answering the following questions for **each topic**
    - What is your target column and what does it represent?
    - Is your target categorical or continuous? is it balanced (Plot the distribution)?
    - What types of features do you have (categorical / numerical)? how many of each ?
    - How many null values does each feature have? what's your plan for each?
    - Which features are most correlated with your target, and with each other?
    - Are there outliers? how would you handle them?
- For reference you could use [Sample Jupyter Notebook](./lightning-talk-notebook.ipynb)

**Slide Presentation**

Your slide presentation should have the following elements for **each topic** you have choosen:
- Your Data Science Problem Statement
- Your goal/aim
- EDA findings :
    - Is this a regression or a classification problem based on your **target** column? 
    - How many missing values ? (if none, mention it)
    - How many categorical and continuous columns do you have?
    - Is there an outlier (If yes mention which column)
    
__You are allocated 5 minutes per person to present__




## Suggested Ways to Get Started
Begin by Asking:
- What is the scope of the need or problem I wish to investigate?
- Who is this for? Who is impacted or affected by this data? Who would benefit from this model? What are my goals for this investigation?
- What does success look like? How will I know if my model performs well?
- Where will I find data for this project? Is the data available?
- What format is the data in? What specific steps do I need to take to access it? How will I explain this project to outside audiences?

Other Tips:

- For your 1st potential topic, start with an idea, then look for potential data that could be used to support that idea.

- For your 2nd potential topic, reverse the process; look for interesting data and then extrapolate problems it could solve and audiences it could impact.

## Useful Resources
- [How to give a good lightning talk.](https://www.semrush.com/blog/16-ways-to-prepare-for-a-lightning-talk/)

### Some ideas for Data Sources
- [Google Datasets Search Tool](https://datasetsearch.research.google.com/)

- [Pandas Data Reader](https://pandas-datareader.readthedocs.io/en/latest/remote_data.html)
- [Jeff's List of Python API Wrappers](https://github.com/discdiver/list-of-python-api-wrappers)
